inline_comments.message: I would debate if these tests are required but if so they can be combined into one test class as they are very small?

I had to write the test for the config class as the coverage check was failing.
We might even exclude the *Config from the coverage check.
What do you say guys??
Is it in second? If yes then use "ttl-time-sec" else "ttl-time-ms" to make it uniform like previous properties.
NB : align with line no 4 and 18

Make it private. 
NB : I prefer to avoid defining new constant but use string literal at line no 50 
getMap("dataSyncEmbeddedCache")

Avoid unnecessary auto-boxing and unboxing :

Integer class is used where we need to treat an int variable like an object. 
Here I can't see any reason to wrapper class as it is overhread at  "mapConfig.setTimeToLiveSeconds(timeToLive);" (Line no 61).

public MapConfig setTimeToLiveSeconds(int timeToLiveSeconds)

Remove name attribute :

name attribute of @Bean annotation is used, when we want to change the default bean name. The default bean name is @Bean annotated method name. Here method name is itself is "dataSyncCache".
Instead of returning Map we can return HazelcastInstance for both data and module sync. and avoid 2 configuration class and keep it common.
We can avoid 2 configuration and create a common CacheConfig for both data and module.

Make it private. 
NB : I prefer to avoid defining new constant but use string literal at line no 50 
getMap("dataSyncEmbeddedCache")
As we write a common configuration it would be merged with module sync.
Can be avoided duplicate/identical (except the Distributed map key) by merging both data and module sync with common configuration.

Can't we use hazelcast-spring as below :

<artifactId>hazelcast-spring</artifactId>
<version>5.1.2</version> 

hazelcast-spring–5.1.2.jar — This is the library to integrate Hazelcast with Spring based applications.

wil there be different config classes for each cache or one for all, this might impact the name.
I am unsure of teh scope of you user story it might need a little groom still? even jsut to agree the scope ie. wich cache are we implementing as part of this first US, do we need to create more US?

agreed, you basically have 2 names for the same thing. A well names string literal should do
e.g. dataSyncSemaphors  (is the scope dat sync and/or module sync?!)
mayb create an embedded cache packae (foler) and then have the havezast config files in there with short names

Version 5.1.2 comes with known vulnerability. So using 4.2.5 only.
hazelcast-spring-4.2.5.jar

Yeah , Even I thought about this but then in the code we need to know the name of the datastore to get the instance of it.

If we go with the existing approach we just get an instance of distributed map directly.

The latest version has known vulnerabilities , Hence sticking to this version for now.
Latest : https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.hazelcast/hazelcast-spring/5.1.2

We are using https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.hazelcast/hazelcast-spring/4.2.5 which looks stable.

not sure why you us the word DataStore

I stil prefer DatSyncSemaphores or similar to indicate the purpose of the values we want to share between the Services instances. 
(maybe be goot to talk in office tomorrow about this :-))

can you explain the function of these in a small walkthrough tomorrow
I also want to question the need to expose (adn possible document) them or will defaults suffice?!
remove 'cache', not consistent with setter below (line #55)

😊
I just had this definition in my mind.
"A distributed data store is a system that stores and processes data on multiple machines."
Term datastore comes from the technology itself.
But I am open to name it differently.

yeah..I can do a code walkthrough with the team.

backupCount : number of backups we want to make.
asyncBackupCount : out of the defined backupCount , how much backups should be made asynchronously.

The reason I am not going this way (at the moment) is I want to keep the configs for Module sync and Data Sync separate.
We are not sure how both of these things will behave.
We might need to tune them separately at a later point in time.
Both stores can need different tuning params.

I know there is little duplication and can be easily extracted out.
But I have this reason for not doing at the moment..
In terms of coverage it is required , otherwise the check fails.

1. Break it inot two "modules-sync" as we are doing for others.
2. Introduce "cluster-name" as well sothat we can override the default name that is "dev" right now.

modules-sync:
    cluster-name: NameOfCluster
We should use "time-to-live-seconds" as it is already defined into MapConfig of hazelcast
Same as modules-sync we should introduce "cluster-name" and rename ttl-sec to  "time-to-live-seconds" sothat it can be autowired with MapConfig
NB : we can merge both ModuleSyncDataStoreConfig and DataSyncDataStoreConfig and give name say "EmbeddedCacheDataStoreConfig" or anything that is more suitable.
As we already defined common configuration into previous it is no more needed.
NB: No need for constants if only using once in the class. Also the method names below will indicate what they being used for.

I suspect here if we get any value into Map ever but always null as .getMap takes "mapConfigName" as key. Here the mapConfigName that we are supliying into initializeDefaultMapConfig method is "moduleSyncSemaphoreConfig" but while accessing using .getMap we are supplied "moduleSyncSemaphore" that never been populated. Please correct my understanding if I missed anything.
Here we should give hazelcast instance name as well to get a logical seperation otherwise hazelcast gives some randon name from MobyNames class that is weird. like awesome_austin, boring_jackson etc.
Remove Qualifier it's redundant here as by default bean name is "moduleSyncSemaphore" only it would able to wired here without it.

We should assert total number of hazelcast instance here by 

com.hazelcast.core.Hazelcast.getAllHazelcastInstances()==2 and to be more specific we should give name of hazelcast instance while creating sothat same instance name can be also asserted with size .
Please read it as *// This is default cluster name that is incorrect.

The configuration name and actual map name can be different.
But for simplicity I can make them same.

you no my usual gripe about constants (names) that dont add value ;-)
I think this is a good example were a string literal is just as good, or better really
bit confused: how is this private method invoked? I something scanning all classes for this?
It is invoked at line no 58 and 47 initializeDefaultMapConfig(...)
I dont mind either way stsm/hazelcast both ar OK
